data.title: Jupiter DEX, ex-Malaysian prime minister shill memecoins in X hack
data.description:
In a series of hacks, decentralized exchange aggregator Jupiter and a former Malaysian prime minister had their accounts hijacked to promote sham coins.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/former-malaysian-prime-minister-x-account-hacked-promote-sham-coin?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Raydium derivatives contracts hit $100 million in daily trading volume within weeks of launch
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Raydium began offering derivatives contracts a few weeks ago, and its daily trading volume has reached $100 million. These contracts allow cryptocurrency traders to speculate on price fluctuations without holding actual tokens.
Currently, Raydium is the third most popular perpetual contract trading platform in the Solana ecosystem, second only to Jupiter and Drift. Although Raydium perpetual contracts are still in their infancy, their growth is still strong; its creators have not invested marketing funds to promote trading tools that have not yet been officially launched. InfraRAY, a core contributor to the project, said: "The Raydium brand is still very influential."
It is worth mentioning that most traders who use the Raydium derivatives trading platform have never visited its website, but accessed its services through trading aggregators, which disperse orders to multiple venues. This means that Raydium's activities may decrease, and crucially, its relationship with direct users (traders) will also weaken. InfraRAY said: "Raydium does a good job as a market maker, but when you build relationships with takers, the network effect is even greater." In addition, Raydium perpetual contract trading is supported by the Orderly Network, which allows perpetual contract traders from multiple blockchains to trade assets on a unified order book. Perpetual contract traders on Solana now contribute 25% of Orderly's total trading volume. CEO Ran Yi said: "Among the dozens of projects that provide perpetual contract trading supported by Orderly, we have a daily trading volume of between $200 million and $400 million." (CoinDesk)

data.title: Former Celsius CEO Alex Mashinsky asks for trial to be postponed a month to May 8
data.description: According to Inner City Press, former Celsius CEO Alex Mashinsky has pleaded guilty to two counts and asked to postpone his trial by a month to May 8, but the U.S. Attorney's Office has not agreed yet.

data.title: StarkWare launches $4 million African blockchain venture fund
Nonprofit group Public Citizen files complaint against Trump for launching TRUMP, questions foreign payment risks
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News - Public Citizen, a U.S. government, business and consumer watchdog, called for a federal investigation into whether Trump's launch of Meme Coin violated laws prohibiting gift solicitation, and is concerned about the existence of untraceable foreign payments.
Public Citizen filed a formal complaint with the Department of Justice and the Office of Government Ethics on Wednesday, claiming that Trump's social media posts were used to promote TRUMP tokens after taking office in January. The Department of Justice, the Office of Government Ethics and Public Citizen did not immediately respond to requests for comment.
Bartlett Naylor and Craig Holman of Public Citizen wrote in the complaint that Trump appeared to be "asking for money for nothing, that is, asking for gifts that benefit him personally." The complaint lists several legal issues surrounding Trump's promotion of Meme Coin, involving multiple overlapping areas of federal law, constitutional provisions and moral standards. (Decrypt)

data.title: Arbitrum Integrates With BitcoinOS To Enhance Bitcoin Network
data.description: According to Odaily, Arbitrum has integrated with BitcoinOS (BOS) to introduce its Layer 2 scaling solution to the Bitcoin network. This integration allows Arbitrum One to function as a hybrid rollup, leveraging Bitcoin's security and Ethereum's scalability. By utilizing BitcoinOS (BOS), the network gains trustless cross-chain capabilities and Bitcoin finality assurance through the BOS Grail bridge. The development team believes this reduces counterparty risk compared to existing Bitcoin interoperability solutions. The BOS framework employs zero-knowledge proofs (ZK) to enable Bitcoin transactions to interact with other ecosystems. This integration is expected to provide Bitcoin holders with broader access to DeFi applications and smart contracts.

data.title: European Central Bank Official Suggests Potential for Further Rate Cuts
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on February 6, European Central Bank Executive Board Member Fabio Panetta indicated that there is still room for interest rate cuts, as inflation is nearing the target level.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 98,000 USDT with a 0.01% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 06, 2025, 06:16 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 98,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 98,021.1875 USDT, with a narrowed 0.01% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Upbit Lists BERA, JTO, and SCR With Trading Restrictions
data.description:
The post Upbit Lists BERA, JTO, and SCR With Trading Restrictions appeared first on Coinpedia Fintech News
Upbit is listing Berachain (BERA), Jito (JTO), and Scroll (SCR) on February 6, with deposits opening earlier. BERA trading starts at 22:00 KST on the Berachain network, supporting staking and transactions via a Proof of Liquidity model. JTO (Solana) and SCR (Scroll) trading begins at...
source: https://coinpedia.org/crypto-live-news/upbit-lists-bera-jto-and-scr-with-trading-restrictions/

data.title: Kraken’s Powell claims in lawsuit he was denied housing due to crypto ties
data.description:
Kraken co-founder Jesse Powell filed a housing discrimination suit, alleging a San Francisco building’s management blocked a sale due to his involvement with crypto.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/kraken-jesse-powell-denied-house-crypto-ties-discrimination-lawsuit?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound
